description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe featuring two large, symmetrical black metal blades with a slightly irregular, rugged edge profile. The blades are mounted on a long, wooden handle that is curved and appears to be wrapped or stained in sections, giving it a weathered, utilitarian look. The central mounting section is reinforced with a black collar or binding, securing the blades firmly to the handle. The handle itself is relatively thick in the middle, tapering towards both ends, and shows visible wood grain and some darker patches, possibly from use or intentional design. The axe has a robust and intimidating appearance, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or display as a decorative or ceremonial piece. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, emphasizing its functional, handcrafted aesthetic.
description: A double-headed black metal axe with a rugged, curved wooden handle and reinforced central mounting. The design is robust and utilitarian, with visible wood grain and a weathered finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, symmetrical black blades and the full length of the curved wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the thickness of the handle, with the mounting collar visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of both blades and the handle's curvature, similar to the front but with minor edge differences.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the blade and handle profile, mirroring view 1 with slight differences in wood grain visibility.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, thick in the middle, tapering at ends, visible grain, matte finish, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed symmetrical blades, black metal, wide crescent shape, rugged edges, no beard, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a black collar reinforcement.
